summary: The Check Changelog Entry job failed because the PR targets v1.16 but contains no changelog entry in .changes/v1.16 (or the 'no-changelog-needed' label).
root_cause: The workflow marked the job failed because the PR would target a v1.16 release and no changelog entry was found in .changes/v1.16 and the 'no-changelog-needed' label was not present.

evidence.quote: ##[error]Currently this PR would target a v1.16 release. Please add a changelog entry for in the .changes/v1.16 folder, or discuss which release you'd like to target with your reviewer. If you believe this change does not need a changelog entry, please add the 'no-changelog-needed' label.
evidence.reason: This is the explicit error message from the CI explaining why the job failed and what is required.
Shows the action explicitly marked the job as failed.
Indicates the action attempted to post/update a comment with failure details about the changelog check.
Path showing the repository workspace where the check ran (context for the failing check).

suggested_fix: Add a changelog entry file under .changes/v1.16 in this PR (or, if appropriate, add the 'no-changelog-needed' label to the PR) so the Check Changelog Entry job can pass.

summary: Dependabot updater exited with an error because it recorded a 'security_update_not_needed' condition for the dependency 'rand', and the updater container failed with exit code 1 while running the update_files command.
root_cause: The Dependabot updater recorded an error of type 'security_update_not_needed' for dependency 'rand' (no security update needed) and then the updater container failed with exit code 1 when running "$DEPENDABOT_HOME/dependabot-updater/bin/run update_files".

evidence.quote: Dependabot encountered '1' error(s) during execution, please check the logs for more details.
evidence.reason: Shows Dependabot reported an error during the update run.
The errors table lists an error with type 'security_update_not_needed'.
Identifies the dependency ('rand') associated with the 'security_update_not_needed' error.

evidence.quote: no security update needed as rand is no longer vulnerable
evidence.reason: Updater log explicitly states that no security update was needed for rand.

evidence.quote: Failure running container 4d986eac50248057b7f3fa031163542ffb36990efbcff66eb3aed0e408c150fa: Error: Command failed with exit code 1: /bin/sh -c $DEPENDABOT_HOME/dependabot-updater/bin/run update_files
evidence.reason: The container running the updater command exited with code 1 while executing the update_files command, causing the job failure.

evidence.quote: For more information see: https://github.com/rust-lang/cargo/network/updates/1333710739 (write access to the repository is required to view the log)
evidence.reason: Provides the Dependabot run URL for detailed logs to investigate the reported error.

suggested_fix: Inspect the Dependabot run log at the provided URL and adjust the updater/config so that 'security_update_not_needed' is handled gracefully (i.e., not treated as a fatal error) or avoid running update_files when no updates are required. Alternatively, update the security-advisory data or Dependabot config for the 'rand' advisory and re-run the Dependabot job.

metadata.provider_error: RuntimeError: diagnosis command exited 1: diagnosis_shim_openai: JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 17 column 16 (char 1094)

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-16 F1 fix: the OpenAI shim emitted _model_info ONLY on successful parse, so post-API failures (JSONDecodeError, RemoteDisconnected) landed in manifests with metadata.model_info=null. The shim now writes a JSON envelope with _model_info even on its error path; this row predates that fix. resolved_model is set to the snapshot ID the canonical 2026-05-13 re-run hit; the actual API call for THIS row may have hit a different snapshot if OpenAI rotated the alias during the run window. base_url_sha256 is the canonical-endpoint hash for documentation; the failing call's actual hash is not recoverable.

summary: The CI job failed while attempting to push a branch to the repository; the git push was rejected due to permission (HTTP 403) for the nextjs-bot account.
root_cause: git push failed because GitHub returned a 403 permission error: 'Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.'

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the failing command was a git push that returned a non-zero exit code.

evidence.quote: remote: Permission to vercel/next.js.git denied to nextjs-bot.
evidence.reason: Explicitly indicates the push was denied due to insufficient permissions for the nextjs-bot account.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Confirms the HTTP 403 response from GitHub when attempting to access the repository.
Shows the failure originated from the sync-react.js script in the repository while performing the push.

suggested_fix: Ensure the workflow uses credentials with push permission for vercel/next.js (for example, update the token/credentials used by nextjs-bot or use a GITHUB_TOKEN/PAT with repo scope) so the git push 'origin update/react/...' can succeed.

summary: A fourslash unit test failed due to an assertion mismatch in a code-fix description during the runtests-parallel step, causing the coverage job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: A fourslash test failed because the expected code-fix description did not match the actual output: the assertion shows an expected/actual mismatch for the 'Add return type' message.

evidence.reason: Identifies the specific fourslash test that was running when the failure occurred.

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to equal 'Add return type \'Foo<string, string[…'
evidence.reason: Shows the exact assertion mismatch: the test expected one code-fix description but the actual description differed.
Indicates the runtests-parallel task failed as a result of the test failure.
Demonstrates the job terminated with a non-zero exit code due to the test failure.

suggested_fix: Either update the test expectation in tests/cases/fourslash/codeFixMissingTypeAnnotationOnExports52-generics-oversimplification.ts to match the actual 'Add return type' description, or change the code-fix output generation to produce the expected description. Re-run the fourslash test locally (run ./built/local/run.js for the test) to verify the fix.
